subject: UX & Research
title: Ontologies and Knowledge Graphs
description: A short lesson to help you apply Ontologies and Knowledge Graphs.

hook.text: Search 'Tom Hanks' on Google. You see his movies, age, spouse, and awards — all connected. That's a knowledge graph.

buildup.text: Ontologies define entities and relationships: Tom Hanks is-an actor, starred-in Forrest Gump, born-in Concord.

discovery.text: Google launched its Knowledge Graph in 2012, connecting billions of facts. Search shifted from words to entities.

twist.text: Building ontologies is expensive. Defining all relationships between all entities in a domain takes enormous effort.

climax.text: AI is automating ontology construction. Machines now extract entities and relationships from text at scale.

punchline.text: Ontologies connect facts into meaning. Knowledge graphs power modern search.

quiz.question: What does a knowledge graph do differently from traditional keyword search?
options: It connects entities and their relationships to understand meaning, not just match words
It searches faster by using a simpler index
It only works for celebrity information
